title: Associate Consultant HR M&A
description:
Job Description

#KGS

KPMG Global Services (KGS) was set up in India in 2008. It is a strategic global delivery organization, which works with more than 50 KPMG member firms to provide a progressive, scalable and customized approach to business requirements

The KGS journey has been one of consistent growth, with a current employee count of nearly 10,000 operating from four locations in India — Bengaluru, Gurugram, Kochi, Pune and Mumbai providing a range of Advisory and Tax-related services to member firms within the KPMG network.

As part of KPMG in India, we were ranked among the top companies to work for in the country for four years in a row by LinkedIn, and recognized as one of the top three employers in the region for women, as well as for policies on Inclusion & Diversity by ASSOCHAM (The Associated Chambers of Commerce & Industry of India).

Team Overview

The position in discussion is with Integration & Separation (I&S), Technology in M&A within the Deal Advisory group at KPMG Global Services (KGS), which is KPMG’s global delivery center and an integral part of the firm’s strategy to enhance innovation, build scalability, and improve profitability globally.


KGS DA&S has been providing solutions to KPMG member firms globally. By leveraging insights and applying best practices, the team helps create customized approaches to M&A transactions. The team’s capabilities include Corporate Finance, Transaction Services, Strategy, Business Modelling, Accounting Advisory Services, Deal Advisory Research and Benchmarking.



Responsibilities

We are recruiting for Associate Consultant in the HR M&A team.

Roles & responsibilities


Working with onshore colleagues to lead and provide support on a wide variety of M&A projects including global programs from a people, workforce analytics and organization effectiveness perspective
Support and sometimes lead work streams on HR I&S projects, supporting client work stream leads to fulfil their work stream objectives
Work with project teams to analyze workforce data by applying key analytical tools to provide key insights and suggest solutions to solve complex client challenges
Demonstrate technical and analytical competence in a deal context for key HR aspects: HR Operations, Labor Relations & Compliance, Organization & Talent Management and Employee Experience and Day 1 comms
Proactively co-ordinate with onshore engagement teams to procure new engagements / engagement work, obtain clarifications, communicate progress and post-delivery de-brief and feedback
Seek ownership for independent engagement and tasks and on larger engagements oversee the work of more junior team members
Assist in the development and presentation of final project deliverables
Provide coaching and development opportunities for junior team members, and act as a mentor to colleagues within the team
Work with cross-functional teams in implementing services, lead team interviews and hold workshops as necessary
When you are not working on a project, you will provide support to senior colleagues in preparation of proposal materials



Qualifications

This role is for you if you have the below

Educational Qualifications


MBA / Bachelor’s degree in a related field from an accredited college/university preferably with major in Human resources



Work Experience


1-3 years of total work experience



Mandatory Technical & Functional Skills


Experience of working on M&A projects including an understanding of HR’s involvement in the deal process is desirable
Experience of working with Powered BI, Alteryx, Tableau
Relevant professional experience, including previous work with a Big 4, global management consultancy firm, or performed an in-house role in which you gained exposure to a large-scale project(s)
Experience working within an HR function including workforce analytics, organization effectiveness, people strategy, organizational design, HR operating model, HR Process efficiency & policy, change management, employee engagement, talent management (Not all specialisms are required and a deep understanding in one area may be sufficient)
Experience of handling multiple stakeholders including ownership of deliverables and good commercial acumen
High quality outputs on Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint. Knowledge or experience on Visio, OrgVue would also be beneficial



Preferred Technical & Functional Skills


Analysis, problem solving and Report writing
A basic understanding of project management methodologies and outputs
Good client relationship skills

title: CA/ACCA Fresher Corporate Treasury
description:
Financial Services Risk Management, Corporate Treasury Advisory – Analyst or ACon




About the Company




KPMG is a global network of professional firms providing Audit, Tax and Advisory services. We operate in 156 countries and have 152,000 people working in member firms around the world. KPMG in India, a professional services firm, is the Indian member firm of KPMG International and was established in September 1993. Our professionals leverage the global network of firms, providing detailed knowledge of local laws, regulations, markets and competition. KPMG in India provide services to over 4,500 international and national clients, in India. KPMG has offices across India in Delhi, Chandigarh, Ahmedabad, Mumbai, Pune, Chennai, Bangalore, Kochi, Hyderabad and Kolkata. The Indian firm has access to more than 7,000 Indian and expatriate professionals, many of whom are internationally trained. We strive to provide rapid, performance-based, industry-focused and technology-enabled services, which reflect a shared knowledge of global and local industries and our experience of the Indian business environment. KPMG Advisory professionals provide advice and assistance to enable companies, intermediaries and public sector bodies to mitigate risk, improve performance, and create value. KPMG firms provide a wide range of Risk Consulting, Management Consulting and Transactions & Restructuring services that can help clients respond to immediate needs as well as put in place the strategies for the longer term. With increasing regulatory requirements, the need for greater transparency in operations, and disclosure norms, stakeholders require assurance beyond the traditional critique of numbers. Hence assurance is being increasingly required on industry issues, business risks and key business processes. The Governance, Risk & Compliance Services practice assists companies and public sector bodies to mitigate risk, improve performance and create value. We assist our clients to effectively manage business and process risks by providing a full spectrum of corporate governance, risk management, and Compliance Services. These services are tailored to meet client’s individual needs, and provide effective support to management in meeting the challenges and opportunities presented by today's complex business environment. Our professionals provide the experience to help companies stay on track and deal with risks that could unhinge their business survival. Our services enable clients to effectively co-ordinate their key growth, quality and operational challenges and working in partnership with us, clients have the benefits of KPMG's experienced, objective, and industry-grounded viewpoints.




Key skills and qualifications

Bachelor degree like Bachelor in Business Administration/ Bachelor in Financial Accounting/ Bachelor in Banking and Insurance from a Tier 1 college/ institute along with FRM (both level cleared) in first attempt and/ or Chartered Financial Analyst from GARP
Strong academic record at 10, 10+2 and graduate examinations
Good understanding of corporate treasury operations and financial products
Understanding of Spot and Derivative market operations for interest rate, foreign exchange products including risk management (high level hedging strategies) and valuation aspects of the same
Well-versed in MS Excel, MS PowerPoint and Power BI. Should be able to build MS Excel based models for risk quantifications using various methods.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Leadership experience at school/college level preferable
High levels of commitment to work




Role and responsibilities

The candidate will be expected to assist assignments involving assisting Indian and international corporate organizations in their treasury operations
The candidate will be expected to assist Assistant Managers to Senior Managers in Corporate Treasury assignments including advisory / Internal Audit/ review of treasury operations areas such as foreign exchange risk management, interest rate risk management, commodities risk management, cash and liquidity management, borrowings management, investments management.
The candidate should be well versed with concepts like Value at Risk and its computational methodologies, Expected shortfall method, Sensitivity analysis, etc. Candidate should understand key concepts of financial risk management.
The assignments would include providing advisory / conducting reviews for the areas mentioned above covering strategy, policy, governance, day to day operations, process improvement opportunities, compliance to internal directives and regulatory requirements etc.
The candidate will be expected to take-up business development initiatives and assisting in creating proposals, responses to RFPs, thought leaderships, etc.
The candidate will be expected to have very strong communication skills and confidence to interact independently with junior to mid-level client personnel.
The position is based out of Mumbai and the candidate must be open to travel




Compensation is competitive with industry standards. Details of the compensation breakup will be shared with short-listed candidates only.




Equal Opportunity Statement




The opportunity is now! If you are interested in being part of a dynamic team, serving clients and reaching your full potential – KPMG Advisory Services is for you!

Are you ready to make an impact in the world of credit risk? Join our dynamic EMEA FIG Developed Markets Credit Risk team, where you'll collaborate with business partners across Global Corporate Banking, Sales and Trading, and Investment Banking. This role offers a unique opportunity to work with both Investment Grade and Non-Investment Grade clients, supporting, analysing and leading diverse credit relationships. Be part of a team that values innovation, collaboration, and excellence.

As a Credit Risk Associate in the Wholesale Credit Risk (WCR) EMEA FIG Developed Markets credit team, you will be responsible for diligence, risk grading, structuring, and ongoing monitoring of a diverse and complex portfolio of Banks and Insurances. You will manage extensive derivatives, securities financing and lending relationships. You will serve as the Credit Officer and primary contact of an actively managed portfolio, while also mentoring junior team members.

Job Responsibilities


Serve as the credit expert for a portfolio of banking and insurance clients in the France and Benelux regions.
Grading and reviewing credit exposures, tracking financial performance and events impacting the assigned portfolio.
Lead credit due diligence for onboarding new clients and reviewing existing ones.
Take a proactive approach in addressing portfolio reviews and deep-dive analyses with internal stakeholders across J.P Morgan departments (e.g. IB, DCM, GCB, Legal)
Produce concise and insightful credit memos, proposing and articulating recommendations for credit actions.
Perform analysis and obtain approval for structured derivatives transactions and lending requests.
Develop knowledge of legal documentation for markets and banking products, taking a proactive approach in supporting the team VP and/or leading negotiations.
Proactively participate in team and department-level projects to improve processes and drive efficiencies.
Review the work of credit analysts and mentoring junior colleagues, while still delivering added value as individual contributor.


Required Qualification, Capabilities And Skills


Strong credit skills and judgment, with a hands-on approach to analysing credit.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, establishing credibility and trust with credit executives, clients, and product specialists.
Strong analytical and numerical skills.
Knowledge of transaction structures, documentation, and banking products to assist with the execution of Lending and Markets’ deals.
High level of energy and ability to prioritise multiple deliverables under time pressure.
Relevant experience in a similar position/function in a comparable environment.


Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills


At least 3 years of experience in financial services (credit risk or similar)
Familiarity with the business models of financial institutions, such as banks and insurances.

Morgan and Chase brands. title: Urgent Hiring - Data Analytics & Data Modelling Professional
description:
Role Overview

We are seeking an experienced Data Analytics & Data Modelling Professional to join our growing Financial Services Technology team. The successful candidate will work on high-impact engagements for major banking and financial services clients, leveraging advanced analytical tools and techniques to extract, transform, query, and analyze large-scale datasets. They will play a pivotal role in delivering data-driven solutions that support risk management, regulatory compliance, customer analytics, product performance, and strategic planning for our clients.

---

Key Responsibilities

Data Analytics & Insights

· Analyze large and complex datasets related to financial and banking products including retail lending, credit cards, mortgages, deposits, treasury, trade finance, and wealth management

· Develop comprehensive analytical reports, dashboards, and presentations to communicate findings and recommendations to senior client stakeholders and leadership teams

· Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA), trend analysis, segmentation analysis, and predictive modelling to support business decision-making

· Identify data patterns, anomalies, and correlations within transactional, behavioral, and financial data

Data Modelling & Architecture

· Design, develop, and optimize logical and physical data models (conceptual, dimensional, and relational) for financial services data environments

· Build and maintain robust data models that support regulatory reporting, risk analytics, customer 360 views, and product profitability analysis

· Ensure data models are scalable, well-documented, and aligned with industry standards.

Database Querying & Management (MySQL)

· Write complex, optimized SQL queries in MySQL to extract, manipulate, and transform large volumes of structured data from relational databases

· Develop and maintain stored procedures, views, functions, and triggers for data processing and automation

· Perform database performance tuning, query optimization, and indexing strategies to enhance data retrieval efficiency

· Manage data extraction pipelines and ensure data integrity, accuracy, and consistency across multiple data sources

description:
Job Description – Assistant Content Manager

Position - Assistant Manager - Content

Job Location - Mumbai

Qualification - MBA / Master’s degree / relevant postgraduate qualification preferred

Role Overview

We are looking for a Content Manager (G4) to lead the design and development of high-quality online and hybrid programs for working professionals across global markets. This role sits at the intersection of curriculum design, instructional quality, and stakeholder management, and plays a critical role in shaping learner outcomes across a diverse portfolio of international programs.




Job Description

Program & Curriculum Development

Lead end-to-end content development for online and hybrid international programs across domains and disciplines.
Translate industry requirements, partner expectations, and learner personas into structured, outcome-oriented curricula.
Define course learning outcomes, session plans, assignments, and assessments aligned to real-world professional and academic outcomes.
Support localization and contextualization of content where required for international audiences and partner institutions.

Instructional Design & Quality

Ensure high standards of instructional design across videos, live sessions, readings, projects, and assessments.
Review and refine content for conceptual accuracy, academic rigor, industry relevance, and learner clarity.
Design learner engagement strategies and assessment approaches in collaboration with Program Managers and academic stakeholders.

Faculty & SME Management

Work closely with academic faculty and industry SMEs to:

define faculty briefs and session objectives
review content outlines, recordings, and deliverables
Build and manage a strong network of faculty, subject matter experts, and industry practitioners across domains relevant to international programs

Cross-functional Collaboration

Partner with Program Managers, Delivery, Sales Enablement, Marketing, and Operations teams to:

meet content production timelines
support learner queries and academic escalations
enable program launches, updates, and ongoing improvements
Coordinate with video, design, and production teams for seamless content execution.

Team & Process Ownership

Contribute to defining scalable content development processes and quality benchmarks for international programs.
Mentor junior content managers, research associates, and external contributors, where applicable.
Drive consistency in content quality, documentation, and execution across multiple partner-led programs.

Must-Have

Strong experience in curriculum design, content development, or learning program management in EdTech, higher education, executive education, or L&D.

Experience working with cross-functional teams and external academic or industry stakeholders.

Prior experience in online/blended learning programs.
Exposure to international higher education or global university partnerships.

Experience working on programs catering to diverse learner cohorts across geographies.

title: Estimator II
description:

As an Estimator II here at Honeywell, you will be responsible for developing accurate and competitive cost estimates for various projects. This role is essential in ensuring the success and profitability of our bids and supporting the growth of our organization.

In this role, you will impact the accuracy and competitiveness of our project bids, ensuring that they align with customer expectations and company standards. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather project requirements and provide detailed cost estimates that support our business objectives.

Your role will also include overseeing, supervising and reviewing tasks performed by team members to ensure effective execution of work; managing end‑to‑end processes and projects for both internal and external clients with responsibility for timely and accurate delivery; issuing clear instructions and directions to team members on tasks to be performed; and mentoring and guiding junior colleagues to support their skill development, professional growth, and overall success

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather project requirements and develop cost estimates
Analyze project plans, specifications, and drawings to determine project scope and identify potential risks
Prepare detailed cost estimates, including labor, materials, equipment, and subcontractor costs

Support the estimation process for medium to large-scale projects

Qualifications

YOU MUST HAVE


2+ years of experience in estimating
Strong knowledge of construction methods, materials, and costs
Proficiency in using estimating software and tools
Ability to interpret project plans, specifications, and drawings


WE VALUE


Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field
Attention to detail and accuracy
Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Continuous learning and professional development mindset

Honeywell helps organizations solve the world's most complex challenges in automation, the future of aviation and energy transition. title: UIUX - Executive
description:
Position: UI/UX Designer

Experience: 1-3 Years

Location: Pune (On-Site)

About Amura

Amura Marketing is a next-generation Martech company that delivers end-to-end marketing and sales solutions to leading brands in India. Our approach combines design, engineering, data, and technology to create digital experiences that are visually engaging, user-friendly, and aligned with business outcomes.

This role is adapted from the structure and expectations of your earlier design JD, but refined for a junior-level UI/UX Designer with stronger visual design capability and relevance to the current AI-enabled design environment.

Role Summary


We are looking for a creative and detail-oriented UI/UX Designer with 1-3 years of experience to join our team in Pune.
The ideal candidate should have a strong foundation in visual design, website and landing page layouting, and basic user experience principles.
This role requires someone who can contribute as a visualiser, meaning the person should be able to convert a creative brief, brand direction, or reference into compelling digital concepts and polished interface designs.
The candidate should be comfortable working on website pages, campaign landing pages, digital assets, and UI layouts, while collaborating with design leads, developers, content teams, and account managers. Familiarity with modern AI-assisted design workflows will be an added advantage.


Key Responsibilities


Visual Design and Creative Execution


Create visually strong and brand-aligned designs for websites, landing pages, microsites, and digital interfaces.

Develop page mockups, section designs, banners, and visual concepts based on project briefs.

Contribute as a visualiser by exploring multiple design directions and converting ideas into polished layouts.

Ensure all designs maintain strong visual hierarchy, typography, colour balance, spacing, and overall composition.


UI/UX Design Support


Prepare wireframes, screen layouts, and interface structures based on business and user requirements.

Apply basic UX principles to ensure clarity, ease of navigation, and usability.

Design layouts that support customer journeys, call-to-action visibility, and responsive behaviour across devices.

Create designs that are responsive, mobile-friendly, and optimised for different screen sizes.

Work under the guidance of senior team members to continuously improve design quality and user experience.


Design Tools and Workflow


Use Figma for wireframing, visual design, prototyping, and collaborative reviews.

Follow established design systems, style guides, and brand guidelines.

Maintain well-structured and organised design files for internal collaboration and development handoff.

Use AI-assisted tools, where relevant, for concept exploration, design ideation, and creative acceleration under team guidance.


Cross-Functional Collaboration


Work closely with UI/UX leads, developers, content teams, and project stakeholders.

Understand project requirements and incorporate feedback in a timely and effective manner.

Support smooth design-to-development handoff by preparing clear and implementation-friendly files.

Participate in design reviews, internal discussions, and project meetings as required.


Quality and Consistency


Ensure consistency in design execution across screens, pages, and digital assets.

Maintain attention to detail in layout structure, content alignment, and responsive design behaviour.

Follow basic accessibility and usability best practices.

Deliver work within timelines and quality expectations.

Required Qualifications


1-3 years of relevant experience in UI design, visual design, web design, or digital creative design.
Strong portfolio demonstrating work in website design, landing pages, campaign layouts, or digital interfaces.
Prior visualiser experience is important for this role.
Good working knowledge of Figma.
Basic understanding of UI/UX principles, responsive design, and digital user journeys.
Good communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.


Must-Have Skills


Strong visual design sense with good understanding of typography, layout, spacing, colour, and composition.
Experience in creating website layouts, landing pages, and digital design mockups.
Good understanding of responsive and mobile-friendly design for websites, landing pages, and digital interfaces.
Ability to work as a visualiser and generate creative design directions from a brief.
Good proficiency in Figma.
Familiarity with AI-assisted design tools such as Lovable, Google AI Studio, Claude, or image generation tools.
Basic understanding of UI components, user flows, and screen structure.
Ability to interpret brand guidelines and apply them consistently across designs.
Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn.


Good-to-Have Skills


Knowledge of Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator.
Exposure to design systems and reusable UI components.
Basic understanding of HTML/CSS for better coordination with development teams.
Understanding of accessibility-friendly design practices.
Prior experience in agency, digital, or Martech environments.


Preferred Candidate Profile


Creative and visually strong, with a good eye for digital aesthetics.
Able to balance visual appeal with usability and clarity.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with multiple projects.
Open to feedback and able to improve designs through iteration.
Interested in learning modern tools, including AI-assisted design workflows.

title: Commercial Finance Analyst
description:
Who We Are

Addepar is a global data and AI platform empowering investment professionals to turn complex financial information into actionable intelligence. Addepar unifies portfolio, market and client data in a total portfolio view and delivers AI-powered insights within investment and client workflows. More than 1,400 firms in nearly 60 countries use Addepar to manage and advise on nearly $9 trillion in assets. Its open platform integrates with nearly 650 software, data and consulting partners to power end-to-end investment operations across firms of all sizes and complexity. Addepar supports clients worldwide with offices in New York City, Salt Lake City, London, Edinburgh, Pune, Dubai, Geneva and São Paulo.

The Role

We’re expanding our Global Finance Operations team in India and building a shared deal governance model that blends financial approvals, deal quality control, and smooth order-to-invoice execution.

This role combines Finance Deal Desk responsibilities (pricing, ARR validation, non-standard deal support) with Order Management responsibilities (contract review, billing readiness, and handoff accuracy).

You’ll be the operational link between Sales, RevOps, Legal, Accounting, and Billing — ensuring deals are structured correctly, approved efficiently, and processed without errors.

This is an ideal role for someone who is hands-on with fundamentals yet capable of stepping into more complex scenarios as the function grows.

Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the country where this role is based on the first day of employment. Visa sponsorship is not available for this position.

What You’ll Do


Deal Desk Responsibilities
Review and validate ARR, pricing, and deal structures in Salesforce CPQ
Ensure accuracy of multi-year, ramped, tiered, or usage-based pricing
Support senior teammates in reviewing non-standard deal constructs (discounting exceptions, blended rates, complex T&Cs, multi-entity deals)
Collaborate with RevOps and Sales Ops to improve quote accuracy and approval compliance
Partner with Legal on commercial terms that impact billing, invoicing, or financial exposure
Evaluate multi-currency pricing using approved FX frameworks
Support credit checks, threshold-based approvals, and finance policy compliance
Order Management Responsibilities
Review contracts and orders for completeness, accuracy, and compliance with internal policies
Ensure all required documents (quotes, contracts, MSAs, addendums) meet billing and invoicing requirements
Validate billing schedules, start dates, terms, and product configurations prior to order activation
Work closely with the Billing and Accounting teams to prevent setup errors or downstream revenue issues
Manage deal-to-invoice readiness, ensuring seamless handoff from CPQ to ERP
Assist in resolving issues related to customer accounts, PO requirements, tax implications, or entity selection
Maintain clean order hygiene across systems, including proactive updates or corrections
Cross-Functional & Operational
Act as a point of contact for Sales on policy questions, deal setup guidance, or required documentation
Analyze recurring deal/order issues and recommend process improvements
Support internal audits or compliance reviews related to contracts, approvals, and billing readiness
Help build scalable workflows as the global deal volume and complexity grow

Who You Are


Minimum 4+ years of experience in Deal Desk, Revenue Operations, Finance Operations, or Order Management within a SaaS or subscription environment
Strong working knowledge of Salesforce CPQ and NetSuite (or similar quote-to-cash/ERP platforms)
Solid understanding of ARR concepts; familiarity with revenue principles is a plus
Ability to independently manage standard deal reviews while supporting complex or non-standard scenarios
Comfort reviewing billing schedules, contract terms, and operational details with high accuracy
Strong cross-functional communication and the ability to influence Sales, Legal, RevOps, and Accounting partners
Analytical mindset, financial judgment, and strong attention to detail
Ability to work in a global, fast-paced, cross-time-zone environment
Growth mindset — comfortable taking on broader responsibilities as the team scales; experience supporting or guiding junior teammates is a plus
[Plus] Experience with multi-currency or international deal structures
[Plus] Exposure to pricing governance or GTM approval workflows
[Plus] Experience in global finance operations or multinational environments


Important Note: This role requires working from our Pune office 3 days a week (Hybrid work model)

Our Values


Act Like an Owner - Think and operate with intention, purpose and care. Own outcomes.
Build Together - Collaborate to unlock the best solutions. Deliver lasting value.
Champion Our Clients - Exceed client expectations. Our clients’ success is our success.
Drive Innovation - Be bold and unconstrained in problem solving. Transform the industry.
Embrace Learning - Engage our community to broaden our perspective. Bring a growth mindset.


In addition to our core values, Addepar is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We seek to bring together diverse ideas, experiences, skill sets, perspectives, backgrounds and identities to drive innovative solutions. We commit to promoting a welcoming environment where inclusion and belonging are held as a shared responsibility.

We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

PHISHING SCAM WARNING: Addepar is among several companies recently made aware of a phishing scam involving con artists posing as hiring managers recruiting via email, text and social media. The imposters are creating misleading email accounts, conducting remote “interviews,” and making fake job offers in order to collect personal and financial information from unsuspecting individuals. Please be aware that no job offers will be made from Addepar without a formal interview process. Additionally, Addepar will not ask you to purchase equipment or supplies as part of your onboarding process. If you have any questions, please reach out to ta-operations@addepar.com.

Job Description

#KGS

KPMG Global Services (KGS) was set up in India in 2008. It is a strategic global delivery organization, which works with more than 50 KPMG member firms to provide a progressive, scalable and customized approach to business requirements

The KGS journey has been one of consistent growth, with a current employee count of nearly 10,000 operating from four locations in India — Bengaluru, Gurugram, Kochi, Pune and Mumbai providing a range of Advisory and Tax-related services to member firms within the KPMG network.

As part of KPMG in India, we were ranked among the top companies to work for in the country for four years in a row by LinkedIn, and recognized as one of the top three employers in the region for women, as well as for policies on Inclusion & Diversity by ASSOCHAM (The Associated Chambers of Commerce & Industry of India).

Team Overview

The position in discussion is with Integration & Separation (I&S), Technology in M&A within the Deal Advisory group at KPMG Global Services (KGS), which is KPMG’s global delivery center and an integral part of the firm’s strategy to enhance innovation, build scalability, and improve profitability globally.


KGS DA&S has been providing solutions to KPMG member firms globally. By leveraging insights and applying best practices, the team helps create customized approaches to M&A transactions. The team’s capabilities include Corporate Finance, Transaction Services, Strategy, Business Modelling, Accounting Advisory Services, Deal Advisory Research and Benchmarking.
KPMG’s Integration & Separation team (ISA) advises clients on executing the operational integrations of acquisitions or joint ventures, and separations of businesses, in the case of divestitures or sale. I&S does this by taking an enterprise‑wide view of M&A deals, across all the functions, to deliver value to our clients. In addition, team is also involved in pre-deal Due Diligence on specific areas of business.

We are recruiting for Consultant in the HR M&A team.

Working with onshore colleagues to lead and provide support on a wide variety of M&A projects including global programs from a people, workforce analytics and organization effectiveness perspective
Support and sometimes lead work streams on HR I&S projects, supporting client work stream leads to fulfil their work stream objectives
Work with project teams to analyze workforce data by applying key analytical tools to provide key insights and suggest solutions to solve complex client challenges
Demonstrate technical and analytical competence in a deal context for key HR aspects: HR Operations, Labor Relations & Compliance, Organization & Talent Management and Employee Experience and Day 1 comms

title: Investment Banking Analyst
description:
Investment Banking Analyst (Graduates and Experienced Hires) - Bengaluru

Please apply to Mumbai on our separate vacancy if that is closer to your location







Our Client

Our subsidiary CF Invest, is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on positions in the investment sector.




We are recruiting on behalf of our client, one of the bulge bracket bank and pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices on an ongoing basis through the first half of each year, with the view of onboarding immediately or upon graduation.




Our client is looking for two professionals to join their Investment Banking team in Bengaluru as an Analyst and use their skills and ideas to make an impact on the success of the bank. You will either be placed in their FIG, TMT or Energy Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry including but not limited to Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications




Graduate – Analyst

Bachelor’s degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations
We consider any degree or subject and although your grade indicates your ability to absorb data and apply it, your degree grade does not influence your chance of applying to us and being interviewed.







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

Analyst annual base salary of 125,000 and up to 150,000 United States Dollars depending on experience. Your salary will be paid in Indian Rupees and bonus in USD
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers
Relocation is provided by the company

Experience working with SQL databases and data modeling.
Experience deploying applications on AWS and/or Azure cloud platforms.

Exposure to AI technologies such as RAG, NLP, LLMs, or agent-based workflows.

Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to navigate ambiguity and deliver end-to-end solutions.

Curiosity and willingness to explore complex enterprise software platforms and emerging technologies.




How you’ll grow




At Deloitte, our professional development plan focuses on helping people at every level of their career to identify and use their strengths to do their best work every day. From entry-level employees to senior leaders, we believe there’s always room to learn. General computer systems

At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.

We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go.

Join EY and help to build a better working world.

About Global Delivery Services

Global Delivery Services refers to EY's worldwide network of service delivery centers. The GDS team plays an important role in EY’s strategy by ensuring effective support to EY’s growth agenda.

Our journey started in 2002 with approximately 200 people. Today we stand at 80,000+ professionals in ten locations around the world. We operate in Argentina, China, Hungary, India, Philippines, Poland, Sri Lanka, Mexico, Spain and the United Kingdom.

Client service is focused on providing Consulting, Assurance, Tax, Strategy & Transactions, and Knowledge support to our clients around the world. The teams enable account teams worldwide to provide seamless, high-quality, value-added support, helping deliver exceptional client service.

Enablement Services provides cost-effective, high-skilled, and innovative services to support EY’s global and local enablement teams. Markets, BMC, AWS, Finance and Accounting, Risk Management, Procurement, People Shared Services, IT Service Delivery and IT Global Infrastructure services, are among the services offered by Enablement Services.

Our innovation specialists serve the GDS Client Service and Enablement Services teams, along with Service Lines, Core Business Services and Sectors. The team brings the desired environment, technologies and skilled teams together for facilitation, rapid prototyping and innovative thinking. The competencies offered include analytics, digital, user experience, mobile technology, infrastructure, Microsoft technologies and open innovation.

The Opportunity

Lead end-to-end HR Operations excellence—from onboarding through offboarding—by driving data integrity, process standardization, and compliance across HRIS, payroll, and benefits. Orchestrate background checks, employment verifications, and HR documentation at scale while building analytics and reporting that inform decisions. Partner with HR, IT, and Compliance to streamline workflows, elevate employee experience, and enable audit readiness. Deliver continuous improvement through SOPs, automation, and KPI-driven governance to reduce cycle times, increase accuracy, and enhance risk controls.

Maintain accurate and compliant employee data within the HRIS, including regular audits, reconciliations, and data integrity checks.

Prepare and issue HR letters and employment verifications, ensuring accuracy, confidentiality, and adherence to HR standards.
Oversee background check coordination and support compliance with labor laws, HR policies, audit requirements, and document retention standards.
Lead process documentation, optimization, and automation initiatives to improve efficiency, consistency, and control across HR operations.

4–6 years HR Operations / HR Shared Services experience
Strong HR process knowledge across the employee lifecycle
Good HRIS experience (Workday, ServiceNow)
Solid background in employee data management and reporting
Experience with HR letters, verifications, and compliance workflows
Strong attention to detail, accuracy, and confidentiality
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
Process improvement mindset with ability to streamline workflows


Ideally, you’ll also


3–5 years HR Operations or HR Shared Services experience
Hands-on experience with HRIS transactions and workflows (Workday, ServiceNow)
Exposure to HR data audits, reconciliations, and reporting
Strong understanding of Hire to Retire HR processes
Solid documentation, analytical, and process mapping skills
Experience supporting process improvements and operational initiative


What We Look For


HR Operations professional with strong exposure across employee lifecycle processes, HRIS transactions, data management, and compliance.
Skilled in HR process execution, data accuracy, documentation, reporting, and workflow coordination across HR platforms and shared services environments.
Experienced in partnering with HR, IT, and cross-functional teams to translate operational needs into scalable, efficient HR processes.
Adept at troubleshooting HRIS issues, managing audits, supporting SLAs, and delivering high-quality operational support in fast paced environments, with strong expertise in data management and HR service delivery.

title: Investment Banking Summer Analyst
description:
This role is for the Bengaluru office. Those across India can apply.







Our Client

Our subsidiary Global Markets Recruitment (“GMR”) is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on emerging and developed markets at entry to middle-management level.




We have been asked to asked by our client to run a campaign to identify entry level interns for this summer 2026. Our client is a bulge bracket bank and one of the pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices. We will run campaigns for them on an ongoing basis through the first half of this year.




Our client is looking for people in their global offices and we will post these roles for each location. Please apply to the location closest to you.




You will either be placed in their FIG or TMT Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry people also in energy, Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst - Intern, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications

Graduate – Analyst

At university studying any degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based including Scientific, Engineering, Arts etc
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets. Thereafter once selected, you will undergo our client one day assessment centre four interviews and one group role play. A decision will be made on the day.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

You will be well compensated for this time and the intern program will last for six weeks. We are running three programs from mid-June, end June and mid-July
Receive a bonus for your time
A permanent offer of employment and part of the Banks’ graduate program
Relocation is provided by the company. Starting salaries will be paid in USD for India $125,000 and up to $150,000 depending on experience
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers

Our AI-powered labor marketplace helps local businesses scale, and enables global technology companies to push the frontiers of robotics and AI. Working Days: 6 Days a Week

Experience: Fresher (0–1 year)

Duration: 3 months

About the Role:

We are looking for a proactive and energetic HR Intern to support our day-to-day recruitment activities. This role is perfect for someone who wants to kick-start their career in HR, enjoys interacting with people, and thrives in a fast-paced startup environment.

Key Responsibilities:


Support end-to-end hiring for fresher and entry-level roles
Source, screen, and call candidates for various positions
Schedule and coordinate interviews with hiring panels
Maintain candidate trackers and ensure timely follow-ups
Assist in releasing offer letters and completing onboarding documentation
Ensure strong communication and coordination between candidates and internal stakeholders
Contribute to improving hiring processes and candidate experience



Requirements:


Good written and verbal communication skills
Strong organizational and follow-up abilities
Proactive attitude and willingness to learn
Comfortable working in a dynamic startup environment
Ability to manage multiple tasks at once
Available to work 6 days a week



Additional information:


Free Lunch + Snacks Daily
Cool workspace and fun team environment
Exclusive project goodies



Our Values


Empathy, Trust & Candor We put ourselves in the shoes of our colleagues and customers and don’t shy away from uncomfortable conversations, instead building trust through honest and direct feedback.
Bias for Action We practice high-velocity decision-making, clear-eyed that we often operate with incomplete information. Growing quickly means it’s OK to be wrong, so long as we learn from our mistakes and course correct!
Always Be Learning We’re a curious bunch, and with AI transforming our workplace we encourage everyone to learn from each other, compounding our knowledge and experience to help us change an entire industry.
Act Like an Owner We work long, hard, and smart, building products that delight our users and drive growth. Your ability to impact Instawork is limited only by your courage and conviction, not your job description.

Our AI-powered platform serves thousands of businesses across more than 50 major markets in the United States and Canada. Forbes included us on their Next Billion Dollar Startups list; RetailTech Breakthrough named us Workforce Hiring Solution of the Year for 2025; and Inc. 5000 recognized us as one of the country's top 10% fastest-growing companies two years in a row. But what matters most is our impact. We're solving real problems for real people, and we’re doing it at scale.

Join our team to help us build something that matters! We’re looking for superstars who want to help us shape the future of work. With hubs in San Francisco, Bangalore, and Chicago, city offices in New York, Phoenix, and Singapore, we're back to working together in-person five days a week because we believe the best ideas happen when great people collaborate face-to-face. We also value diverse perspectives and encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds.

Ready to make an impact? Learn more at www.instawork.com/about.

title: Program Associate (Talent and Partnerships)
description:
Avistrat works with aerospace engineers and organisations to build design capability and deploy engineering capacity. We engage with industry across two dimensions of scale:




Vertical scale — by deepening technical capability, and execution layers within specific aerospace domains.
Horizontal scale — by expanding across teams, programmes, and partner organisations where credible engineering capacity is required.




On the capability side, we support organisations by augmenting their design engineering functions and strengthening technical depth through structured, project-oriented engagement.

On the capacity side, we work closely with industry partners to identify, prepare, and deploy engineering talent aligned to real-world aerospace requirements.




Our work sits at the intersection of:




Engineering design
Industry readiness
Talent deployment




We could be called an EdTech, SkillsTech or an engineering services company but are building Avistrat as a scalable, integrated aerospace company — one that grows in depth, breadth, and responsibility while maintaining high standards of quality, credibility, and long-term partnership.

This is a high-rigour, outcome-driven environment where judgment, ownership, and execution matter more than volume or optics.




ROLE OVERVIEW




This is an entry-level, execution-focused role providing hands-on exposure to how talent pipelines and client delivery are executed in the aerospace domain.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES




Candidate Intake & Decision Support:




- Own the first line of evaluation for candidates engaging with Avistrat

- Conduct structured conversations to assess readiness, seriousness, and fit

- Build judgment on who should move forward and who should not

- Prepare concise recommendations that directly inform leadership decisions




Talent Pipeline Ownership:




- Build and manage a live talent pipeline aligned to industry needs

- Shortlist candidates for internships, entry-level roles, and capacity-building mandates

- Coordinate execution from shortlisting to interview outcomes

- Track progress, identify bottlenecks, and close gaps proactively




Client & Partner Interface (Execution-Led):




- Support delivery of client capacity-building mandates

- Coordinate candidate–client interactions and timelines

- Gain exposure to real industry-facing execution with increasing responsibility




Process Building & Continuous Improvement:




- Convert live execution into repeatable processes and templates

- Analyse and improve tracking of candidates, conversations, and outcomes

- Contribute to making Avistrat’s talent pipeline scalable and predictable




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT




Not a sales role
Not a call-centre counselling role
Not an HR Admin role




WHAT WE LOOK FOR




A background and interest in the People or Human Resources function (academic or professional) is a must
Experience or demonstrated comfort with recruitment is a must
Some experience with aerospace would be ideal
Clear verbal and written communication
Comfort with ownership and follow-through
Ability to learn by doing and exploring fresh ideas
Ability to make and defend structured judgements
Attention to detail and professional conduct
Growth and improvement mindset
Drive to achieve goals




WHAT YOU WILL LEARN




How to thrive in a multi-stakeholder work environment
How to manage trade-offs for strategic decision-making
How quality of talent directly affects delivery outcomes
What enables successful client engagement
What the various elements of business operations are
How to grow and take ownership to drive work streams




GROWTH PATH




Freshers and early hires will join as interns for the first 3 months. Subsequently, the growth path could be expected as below:




0–6 months: Junior Talent and Partnerships Programme Associate (execution and judgment building)
6–12 months: Expanded ownership of intake, pipeline, and client delivery
12–18 months: Eligibility for progression to lead role, based on performance and business needs




There are various possible avenues along which career progression can be made based on candidate aspirations and business needs:




Ownership of the talent pipeline of Avistrat and organisational HR management
Programme management to manage a business function within Avistrat’s operating structure
Founder’s Office team to gain first-hand entrepreneurship insights




For further details about us, please visit our website: www.avistrat.com

title: Product Demonstration Specialist
description:
About Elevatoz Loyalty

Elevatoz Loyalty is a technology-led loyalty and engagement company that partners with leading businesses to drive sustainable sales growth through smarter channel engagement, rewards management, communication, and program execution. We design and operate high-impact loyalty ecosystems for channel partners, retailers, influencers, and other commercial stakeholders across industries. Our approach integrates technology, strategic consulting, servicing excellence, and operational rigor to deliver programs that are commercially effective, scalable, and deeply engaging for all participants.




Executive Summary

We seek an entry-level solution demo specialist (product demonstration specialist) to join Elevatoz Loyalty’s sales team in Bengaluru. This client-facing role suits a recent graduate (BCA or B.Sc. in Computer Science/IT) who is confident and tech-savvy. You will learn about our offerings in depth and deliver engaging software demonstrations, tailoring each demonstration to the prospect’s needs. Strong communication, enthusiasm towards talking to new people, and an eagerness to learn are essential for this position.




Role Overview

Conduct product and solution demonstrations for prospective clients and internal stakeholders.
Understand Elevatoz Loyalty’s platform features, modules, and use cases thoroughly.
Tailor demo narratives to different industries, client needs, and business contexts.
Collaborate with the sales and solution teams to align on demo objectives and outcomes.
Ensure every demo is polished, well-structured, and highlights real business value (not just features).
Continually improve demo scripts, environments, and presentation flow based on feedback.
Support internal teams with demo readiness, documentation, and assets.
Stay up-to-date with product updates and help standardize demo journeys for key solutions.




Key Responsibilities

Demo Delivery: Present clear, compelling live demos (in-person and virtual) of the Elevatoz Loyalty platform to prospects.
Customization: Adapt each demonstration to the client’s sector, size, and requirements, focusing on relevant use cases.
Collaboration: Work closely with sales, solution engineers, and product teams before demos to understand client goals.
Storytelling: Use strong storytelling and real-world examples to explain how our software solves client problems.
Value Emphasis: Go beyond features by emphasizing the practical business benefits of the solution.
Feedback Loop: Gather client feedback after demos and suggest enhancements to scripts and presentations.
Preparation: Assist in creating demo assets (slides, scripts, sandbox accounts) and ensuring the demo environment is ready.
Knowledge Building: Maintain deep knowledge of platform capabilities, industry trends, and competitor offerings.




Desired Candidate Profile

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, Electronics, or a related field (e.g., BCA, B.Sc.). 
Experience: Fresh graduate or 0–1 year experience (internship experience in pre-sales, support, or tech roles is a plus).
Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills; clear and polished presentation style.
Technical Understanding: Comfort with learning software platforms; ability to explain technology in simple, business-friendly language.
Confidence: Poised and self-assured in client-facing situations; able to handle questions and drive discussions.
Preparation & Storytelling: Strong preparation skills, logical organization, and an ability to craft a coherent demo narrative.
Attention to Detail: High level of professionalism and attention to detail in presentations and documentation.
Teamwork: Collaborative attitude and willingness to work with multiple stakeholders (sales, product, customer success).




Skills & Attributes

Presentation Skills: Proficiency with presentation tools (PowerPoint or similar) and demo software
Software Savvy: Basic familiarity with SaaS platforms or enterprise software environments.
Structured Thinking: Methodical approach to planning demos and addressing client needs step by step.
Client Orientation: Business-focused mindset; always relate features back to client outcomes.
Adaptability: Quick learner who can adjust to new product updates and client feedback.
Reliability: Consistency and polish in demo execution and follow-ups.




Why Join Us

High Impact Role: Be the face of our product in a high-visibility role that directly influences client decisions.
Learning & Growth: Rapidly develop skills in SaaS platforms, sales processes, and enterprise solutions by working closely with our sales and product experts.
Exposure: Engage with enterprise clients and strategic sales cycles, shaping how prospects perceive our platform and solutions.




Location & Employment Type

Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.
Employment Type: Full-time position.

Position Summary

Analyst - Executive Accelerators – Client Experience Delivery

About Executive Accelerators

Are you striving for a high-performing role where your efforts tangibly support the relationship-building activities of Deloitte’s client teams with the C-Suite executives of our most important clients? Are you interested in taking your current skills to a new level to meet the demands of this role? The Client Experience Delivery (CED) team, a specialized sub-team within the Executive Accelerators (XA), works with the role-based CXO Programs to support Next Generation CXO Event development, operations and execution. On behalf of the CED leadership, the Academies leader will oversee the production support, logistics and event execution for event programs and also drive innovation, process improvement, and leading practices with a focus on delivering consistent, high-quality client experience for all event programs.

Manage Salesforce requests: validate data and customize extracts for leadership reports.

Monitor Event Coordinator mailbox for requests.

Review travel manifest and confirm transportation updates.
Collaborate with ServiceNow team to process and track invoices.

Advanced skills in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint): data cleansing, analysis, and template management.
Experience with Salesforce, Teams, and SharePoint.
Proficient in secondary research (e.g., sourcing executive bios from LinkedIn and company websites).
Basic editing and proofreading abilities.
Multitasking ability to manage multiple projects accurately and on time, with clear stakeholder communication.
Self-motivated team player who works independently, with a client-focused, detail-oriented approach.

Event management experience is a plus
Knowledge of managing finance applications and processing invoices is an advantage.
Appreciation for working in a consultative, team-oriented environment.

title: Associate Scrum Master
company: Advance Auto Parts India (India Innovation Center)
description:
Job Description

About Advance Auto Parts

Founded in Roanoke, VA in 1932, Advance Auto Parts is a leading automotive aftermarket retail parts provider that serves both professional installer and do-it-yourself Customers. As of July 13, 2019, Advance operated 4,912 stores and 150 Worldpac branches in the United States, Canada, Puerto Rico, and the U.S. Virgin Islands. The Company also serves 1,250 independently owned CARQUEST branded stores across these locations in addition to Mexico, the Bahamas, Turks, and Caicos and the British Virgin Islands. The company has a workforce of over 70,000 knowledgeable and experienced Team Members who are proud to provide outstanding service to their Customers, Communities, and each other every day.

About Advance India Innovation Center (AIIC)

We are continually innovating and seeking to elevate the Customer experience at each of our stores. For an organization of our size and reach, today, it has become more critical than ever, to identify synergies and build shared capabilities. The Advance India Innovation Center (AIIC), located in Hyderabad, is a step in this strategic direction that enables us to access a larger talent pool, unlock operational efficiencies and increase levels of collaboration.

Job Profile Summary

As an Associate Scrum Master, you’ll support IT teams in delivering high-quality technology solutions using Agile methodologies. This entry-level role is ideal for individuals with a foundational understanding of Scrum who are eager to grow into a full Scrum Master position. You’ll work closely with cross-functional teams to facilitate Agile ceremonies, remove blockers, and ensure smooth sprint execution.

Key Responsibilities


Assist in facilitating Agile ceremonies including daily stand-ups, sprint planning, sprint reviews, and retrospectives.
Support the Scrum Master in managing sprint backlogs and tracking team progress using tools like Jira.
Help identify and remove impediments to ensure team productivity and delivery.
Promote Agile principles and foster a culture of continuous improvement within IT teams.
Collaborate with developers, QA engineers, business analysts, and product owners to ensure alignment on sprint goals.
Maintain Agile artifacts and ensure transparency of team progress to stakeholders.
Monitor sprint metrics (velocity, burndown charts, etc.) and assist in reporting.
Participate in Agile training and coaching sessions to enhance team maturity.


Qualifications


First or second level ScrumMaster certification (CSM, PSM I, II).
Experience in the ScrumMaster role for at least two years in a tech shop environment.
A solid baseline of tech literacy is expected; hands-on experience at coding/software development will be preferred.
Solid knowledge and understanding of Scrum terminology: servant leadership, facilitation, situational awareness, conflict resolution, continual improvement, empowerment, and increased transparency.
Demonstrated ability to apply common sense to "intentional gaps" in the framework.
Some understanding of DevOps and cloud concepts including CI/CD, auto-scaling, automated and manual testing methodologies, pairing, etc.
Experience with project management software and diagramming tools.
Working knowledge of non-Scrum Agile approaches: XP, Kanban, Crystal, FDD, etc.
Some experience as a team lead will be preferred.
Excellent communications skills – written, verbal and visual.
High energy and solid work ethic, with motivational and leadership qualities.


Education And Experience


Bachelor's degree in computer science, Information Systems, or a related field.
Overall Work Experience: 5-7 years (at least 2 years’ experience in and around data analytics and engineering )

The Founder’s Office – GTM Associate is a high-intensity track built around real ownership, steep learning curves, and close collaboration with founders and senior leaders — compressing ~5 years of career growth into ~2.

You’ll work on high-impact initiatives across growth, strategy, customer engagement, and market expansion. Top performers quickly take on ownership, lead teams, and drive initiatives across geographies. This is not a conventional entry-level job. This is a launchpad for future business leaders.

Work directly with founders and senior leaders on GTM strategy, execution, and experimentation
Drive market expansion and international go-to-market initiatives
Own cross-functional projects across sales, marketing, customer success, partnerships, and product
Lead revenue growth and funnel optimization initiatives
Analyze markets, customers, and performance data to identify growth opportunities
Engage with global customers to uncover pain points and build customer-first solutions
Support and lead new launches, pilots, and strategic projects
Collaborate across geographies and functions to deliver outcomes
Take end-to-end ownership of initiatives and be accountable for results
Over time, you will move from: Execution → Ownership → Market/Function Leadership → Team Leadership / P&L Responsibility
Our expectation is clear: 




Within ~2 years, high performers will be operating at a team-lead level. This program is built for people who want accelerated responsibility, not gradual hand-holding.




Who we're looking for

We seek outliers and you might be a fit if you have:

Undergraduate degree in any discipline from a Tier 1 institution, completed by June 2026 (or final examinations completed by then). Open to recent graduates with 0–2 years of experience
Willingness to commit to a minimum of two years with the organization
Strong spoken and written English communication
High learning agility, curiosity, and initiative-taking ability
Entrepreneurial, ownership-driven mindset—you see problems as opportunities
Strong business aptitude, logical reasoning, and comfort with numbers and data
Structured, strategic thinking with exceptional attention to detail
Proven problem-solving skills and customer empathy
Passionate hustler mentality with the drive to execute, not just plan
Excited by ambiguity, high expectations, and real responsibility
Aspiration to become a future business leader, not just a functional specialist
Hunger to work on cutting-edge, industry-leading problems
Bonus: Proficiency in a foreign language




About DeltaX

Founded in 2012, DeltaX is an AI & Automation company driving transformation primarily in the area of Marketing & Advertising. Our flagship product is a data-driven digital advertising platform designed for agencies and advertisers to seamlessly plan, create, buy, track, attribute, and report campaigns across search, social, display RTB, mobile, video, and other digital channels. Its cutting-edge technology empowers marketing teams worldwide with deep insights, automated ROI-based spend optimisation, and the ability to execute award-winning, technology-driven campaigns. The scope of our offering has compounded in width and depth with AI and automation being at the forefront of it.

Interviews
Offer Roll-out
